fix: revert to clean next.config and build script; Vercel now uses outputDirectory

Remove distDir hack and --webpack flag. The correct fix is Vercel project-level:
- rootDirectory cleared (was: frontend)
- installCommand: cd frontend && npm install
- buildCommand: cd frontend && npm run build
- outputDirectory: frontend/.next

With outputDirectory set, Vercel's @vercel/next post-build validator correctly
looks for routes-manifest-deterministic.json in frontend/.next/ instead of the
repo-root .next/ path it used when rootDirectory was set.

commit.message:
refactor: rebuild auth gate from scratch

- Remove unconditional boot() call that connected WebSocket before auth
- Remove checkAuthRequired() that silently bypassed auth when api_keys empty
- initAuth() now ALWAYS shows login on first load
- validateStoredToken() validates existing JWT against /whoami before boot
- boot() is only called after a confirmed valid session
- patchFetch no longer injects Bearer on /auth/login requests
- patchWebSocket uses constructor pattern (avoids prototype issues)
- Named handleSubmit so it can be removed after login

Fixes: login showed 'Connected' immediately, wrong creds appeared to succeed,
       form submit had no handler when api_keys not configured

commit.message: Inspector CLI (#18229) This change introduces the Inspector CLI. High level, the CLI includes a long lived node process for the "bridge", a short lived node process for the CLI (which communicates with the bridge), and a new `StartInspectable` API that runs in the browser/app side and connects to the bridge. `StartInspectable` uses parts of the Inspector framework, like the ServiceContainer for modularity/extensibility. Services can introduce additional commands, which are dynamically fetched from the CLI. The "inspectable" ServiceContainer is longer lived than the one in Inspector, and the one in Inspector is aware of the "inspectable" one, which means Inspector can launch the "inspectable" if needed, can expose bridge connection status, and even contribute more CLI commands. - Add the ability for `ServiceContainer` to have a "parent" `ServiceContainer` (longer lived). - Add `StartInspectable` that sets up a `ServiceContainer`with a set of services that are browser independent and provide a set of CLI commands (query scene entities, query entity details, take a screenshot, get stats, get shader code, etc.). - `ShowInspector` now calls `StartInspectable`, which is ref counted so if it has already been called, it just returns the existing instance. The inspectable `ServiceContainer` is used as a "parent" container for the Inspector `ServiceContainer`. - There is a new Inspector service that monitors the CLI "bridge" connection status and shows an icon in the bottom right toolbar, plus shows connect/disconnect toasts. - Added a CLI and CLI bridge (using WebSockets). There are a few fixed commands, like --help and --sessions, but everything else is dynamic and the commands are determined by the browser/app side. - Updated Inspector readme.md files to call out the CLI (largely for AI agent discoverability). - Updated the core readme.md (@babylonjs/core + babylonjs umd) with a tiny note about the Inspector (another breadcrumb for AI agents). - Updated the @babylonjs/inspector to fully bundle the CLI and the bridge, including all the node WebSocket code. This way it is very easy to run the CLI without having any external dependencies. - Marked new Inspectable related APIs as @experimental for now. When developing the Inspector CLI, you can run `npm run babylon-inspector -- --help` (or other commands) from the `packages/dev/inspector-v2` directory. If you've built the full monorepo, you can also run `npx babylon-inspector --help` (or other commands) from anywhere, which will pick up the final built CLI from the public package. This is the same way someone consuming the @babylonjs/inspector package would run the CLI. There is still an open question about whether we should have an Inspector MCP instead of or in addition to the CLI itself (most of the code would still stay the same though). Need to follow up with @RaananW on this. After this PR, I will also explore more the idea of calling `StartInspectable` by default in Sandbox and Playground, and also the idea of exposing additional tool specific CLI commands, like getting/setting Playground code for example. We need some more discussion around this to see how it relates to other tool MCP efforts, so this will be a possible future change. ``` [04/02 11:12:27] /Users/ryantremblay/Repos/Babylon.js/packages/dev/inspector-v2> npm run babylon-inspector -- --session 2 --command get-count-stats > @dev/inspector@1.0.0 babylon-inspector > node --no-warnings dist/cli/cli.js --bridge-script dist/cli/bridge.js --session 2 --command get-count-stats { "totalMeshes": 13, "activeMeshes": 13, "activeIndices": 1631379, "activeFaces": 543793, "activeBones": 6, "activeParticles": 30, "drawCalls": 2212014, "totalLights": 2, "totalVertices": 274644, "totalMaterials": 10, "totalTextures": 20 } ``` --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: implement flood defenses — per-conn ratelimiter, ping ban, message gate

Four network-layer flood defenses against the tokio starvation attack (AV-14.11):

1. Per-connection rate limiter (server.rs)
   - handle_peer() now creates a local RateLimiter::new() that shadows the
     shared Arc<RwLock<RateLimiter>> parameter.
   - Eliminates the cross-peer write-lock contention that previously caused
     tokio thread starvation at 50+ simultaneous peers; each connection's
     rate checks are now fully independent.

2. Pre-channel message gate (server.rs, TLS + plaintext bridge tasks)
   - Both I/O bridge tasks now count raw messages per second before
     forwarding to the msg_read_tx channel (before deserialization).
   - Soft limit 200 msg/s: silently dropped (no channel send).
   - Hard limit 500 msg/s: sends Err('Message flood detected') to trigger
     record_violation() + record_message_flood() in the main loop, then
     closes the bridge task (disconnects the peer).

3. Ping flood escalation (server.rs)
   - ping_excess_streak counter: 3 consecutive pings that exceed the soft
     rate limit now call blacklist.record_violation() and
     ai.attack_detector.record_ping_flood().
   - If the violation count crosses the ban threshold, the peer is
     disconnected immediately.
   - Previously: excess pings silently dropped, peer never banned.

4. PingFlood + MessageFlood in AttackDetector (attack_detector.rs)
   - New AttackType variants: PingFlood, MessageFlood.
   - New fields: ping_flood_times, message_flood_times (sliding-window maps).
   - record_ping_flood(): ≥3 events in 10s → BlockPeer.
   - record_message_flood(): ≥2 events in 60s → BlockPeer (Critical severity).
   - Both feed the 30s enforcement loop → IPBlacklist auto-ban.

Fixes AV-14.11 (reconnection-storm tokio starvation) attack surface.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix corrupt GLB output: strip Draco metadata and consolidate data URIs after decompression

decompress_gltf() from gltf-draco-transcoder decompresses mesh binary data
but leaves KHR_draco_mesh_compression in the JSON metadata and stores
decompressed data as base64 data URIs in separate buffers. This produces
internally inconsistent GLBs that Godot cannot import.

Added _strip_draco_extension_from_glb() which:
- Removes KHR_draco_mesh_compression from extensionsUsed/Required
- Removes the extension from mesh primitive definitions
- Consolidates data-URI buffers back into the binary chunk
- Produces a spec-compliant single-buffer GLB

commit.message:
fix: set distDir='../.next' on Vercel to match post-build validator path

Vercel's post-build validator (introduced in recent @vercel/next update)
looks for routes-manifest-deterministic.json at /vercel/path0/.next/.
With rootDirectory:frontend, the build runs from /vercel/path0/frontend/
and outputs to frontend/.next — one directory below where the validator
looks. Setting distDir='../.next' when VERCEL=1 redirects the build output
to /vercel/path0/.next/ so the validator finds the file.

Locally VERCEL env var is not set so distDir defaults to .next (normal).

Also squashes the empty trigger commit into this real fix.

commit.message:
feat: replace 1-hour JWT auth tokens with permanent HMAC autologin codes

Newsletter links now use HMAC-based autologin codes (?ac=<hex>) instead of
Firebase custom tokens (?at=<jwt>). Key improvements:

- Never expires: HMAC code is deterministic, client exchanges it for a fresh
  Firebase token on-demand via Cloud Function API call
- Much shorter URLs: 67 chars (HMAC hex) vs 793 chars (JWT) — well under
  Mailgun's 1000-char click tracking limit
- No Firebase Admin SDK needed at send time: pure HMAC, no async token
  generation per subscriber

Changes:
- scripts/send-newsletter.mjs: Replace getOrCreateCustomToken() with
  generateAutologinCode() using HMAC('autologin:'+email, secret)
- functions/src/newsletterSubscriptionManagement.js: Add exchange_auth_code
  action that validates HMAC and returns fresh createCustomToken()
- functions/index.js: Support JSON responses from exchange_auth_code
- functions/src/newsletterConfirmationEmail.js: Remove JWT from confirmation
  URLs (Cloud Function already returns token in API response)
- App.tsx: Auto-login useEffect exchanges ac code via API; action handler
  supports ac for unsubscribe/resubscribe; skip auto-login for confirm action
- services/newsletterSubscribers.ts: Add exchangeNewsletterAuthCode()
- services/authService.ts: Check ac param in shouldWaitForAuth()
- build-plugins/constants.ts: Add ac to SPA redirect script

Backwards compatible: legacy at/authToken params still work for old emails.

commit.message:
watchdog: distinguish RPC busy from de-registration; add audit sections 14.11-14.12

- RPC timeout no longer immediately counts as de-registration.
  If timed has logged within DAEMON_ACTIVE_SECS (default 90s), the
  daemon is alive but busy (fork resolution, reconnection storm).
  Busy timeouts increment rpc_busy_streak; only after RPC_BUSY_MAX
  (default 10 consecutive polls, ~90s) without log activity does it
  escalate to a fail_streak increment and eventual restart.
- When busy: logs '⏳ RPC timeout — daemon is alive and logging
  (busy_streak: N/10); NOT counting as de-registration'
- When silent AND unresponsive: logs 'RPC call failed — daemon has
  NOT logged in 90s' and escalates normally.
- RPC_TIMEOUT default: 3s → 8s (more headroom during load spikes)
- FAIL_THRESHOLD default: 2 → 3 (confirmed not-active before restart)
- daemon_recently_active() function added (journalctl short-unix parse)
- rpc_busy_streak state variable + reset on RPC success
- Restart log message: 'detected' → 'confirmed' to reduce false alarm tone
- New --rpc-busy-max N and --daemon-active-secs N CLI flags
- docs: add sections 14.11 (tokio starvation via reconnection storm)
  and 14.12 (watchdog false-restart on RPC timeout) to security audit;
  add three new rows to summary attack surface table

Fixes the pattern seen Apr 8 2026: restarts #12-17 at 10-min intervals
triggered by RPC starvation from the fork-resolution busy-loop.

commit.message:
Feat/phase 3 ecommerce (#7)

* feat: implement Phase 3 e-commerce heuristics and related product pruning

* docs: modernize OUTPUT_CONTRACT and EXTRACTION wiki references

* feat(core): modernize extraction engine, eradicate panics, and add cloud jina opt-in

This commit resolves major architectural goals by hardening execution stability,
improving text-extraction heuristics, and enforcing privacy-by-default routing:

1. Panic Eradication & Error Handling
   - System-wide removal of `unwrap()` and `expect()`, replacing them with safe
     `Result` propagation.
   - Refactored `RipwebError` and domain errors (`DdgError`, `GithubError`, etc.)
     to leverage the `thiserror` crate.
   - Updated `main()` to return `anyhow::Result<()>`, migrating `std::process::exit()`
     calls to graceful `anyhow::bail!` error bubbling.

2. AST-Based Semantic Pruning
   - Replaced fragile character-counting link saturation heuristics with mathematical
     AST calculations via the `tl` crate (`evaluate_block_saturation`).
   - Accurately computes link density by evaluating structural DOM nodes,
     significantly improving the accuracy of sidebar and navigation pruning.

3. Privacy by Default
   - Added an explicit `--allow-cloud` CLI flag to gate third-party
     extraction proxies (Jina.ai).
   - Forces `ripweb` to strictly adhere to localized parsing and heuristic fallbacks
     unless explicitly given permission to use offsite JS rendering engines.

Also updates `CURRENT_PRIORITIES.md` to mark these milestones as completed.

commit.message:
Switch scheduled Pages deployment from concurrent git pushes to single artifact-based deploy (#16)

* Initial plan

* Switch scheduled workflow to artifact-based GitHub Pages deployment

Replace concurrent gh-pages git pushes with the recommended Actions
Pages pattern: build jobs run in parallel and upload artifacts, a
single deploy job downloads all artifacts and publishes once via
actions/configure-pages + actions/upload-pages-artifact +
actions/deploy-pages.

- schedule.yaml: set permissions (pages:write, id-token:write,
  contents:read), add concurrency group, pass publish:false to build
  jobs, add single deploy job
- build.yaml: update permissions, replace git-push publish job with
  deploy-pages-based publish (for standalone workflow_dispatch use)

fix(smoketest): prevent SIGPIPE from trivy killing script via EXIT trap

trivy --version outputs many lines; head -1 closes the pipe early causing
trivy to get SIGPIPE (exit 141). With set -eo pipefail this non-zero exit
fires the EXIT trap inside the $() subshell which calls cleanup/exit 0,
terminating the whole script. Add '|| true' to suppress the SIGPIPE.
